Off Calendar Dec-15-2022 Set For Trial On Mar-18-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS DIGNITY HEALTH and COMMONSPIRIT HEALTH'S Motion For Change Of Venue.

Defendant's "motion for change of venue" is denied.

Defendant concedes that its "corporate office is located" in "San Francisco;" thus, venue is proper here. (Memo. 3:26; CCP 395.5.) Citing CCP 397(c), defendant argues "the convenience of the witnesses" for a venue change. (Memo. 3:2-3, 5:2-3.) The argument founders on two points. First, barring extraordinary circumstances not claimed here, convenience of defendant's employees is not considered on a CCP 397 motion. (Wrin v. Ohlandt (1931) 213 Cal. 158, 160; Lieberman v. Sup. Ct. (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 396, 401; Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d Supp. 11, 17.) Second, the motion must be supported by declarations showing the names of non-party witnesses, each's expected testimony and facts showing inconvenience for each. (Id.) Defendant does not carry that burden. (See Memo. 5:8-11; Goulding Dec. 2:17; Arias Dec. 2:5-9.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
